translated: It is incredibly difficult, but you just have to find a way to resolve those feelings within yourself.
Having that competitive spirit can lead to your own motivation,
and I personally want to be the kind of person who uses that as energy to work harder.
I feel that way quite often myself.
Like, "She is so good..." and things like that. Seriously.
Every single day, really.
It happens.
But on the flip side, Shizuku said something she really shouldn't have said to the girls who were seriously aiming for that center position.
She ended up blurting it out.
Of course, she didn't mean any harm. I think everyone who knows Shizuku's personality understands that.
It wasn't malicious, but Shizuku was so overwhelmed at the time that she could only see what was right in front of her.
She was in a period where she could only focus on herself, so she accidentally said, "I think there are other girls more suited for this center spot."
It just came out.
I understand that feeling too.
I mean, the pressure of being the center is insane.
There are expectations and how you present yourself, and above all, Shizuku wasn't being her true self.
She had a persona that she only maintained thanks to everyone else,
which made it even harder.
That was part of it too, and honestly,
this whole situation is just so raw.
It really is.
To be so young and sensitive, and then have your worth decided by whether someone else chooses you... that's just cruel.
You can't become the center just by working hard.
It's tough because you can't get there unless someone else recognizes you and says, "You are the center."
But I also feel like that's exactly why these girls are able to shine so brightly.
Reading this scenario made me think I should really be careful myself, but because she went through this rough patch,
we have the Shizuku we know today. Without this, Momojan wouldn't have been born.
I feel very grateful for that as well.
Shizuku was saying "thank you" too. She was truly, truly grateful.
And Arisa-chan... she stuck to her guns right until the very end.
